changesProposed.name: Alpha School

verified.name: Alpha School
verified.description: A private K-12 school network founded in 2014 that uses a proprietary '2 Hour Learning' instructional model combining software-based AI tutoring with life skills workshops. Students complete core academics in approximately two hours daily and spend afternoons on workshops and experiential learning.
verified.notes: Wikipedia source [2] confirms 13 campuses as of April 2026 across Arizona, California, Florida, New York, Texas, and Virginia. Tuition ranges $10,000-$75,000 per year per source [2]. Joe Liemandt identified as principal in source [2]. Database entry references '~$1B Liemandt commitment' but no specific funding amount is verified in provided sources. Founded as spinoff of Acton Academy, formerly called Emergent Academy per source [2].

changesProposed.name: Fervo Energy

verified.name: Fervo Energy
verified.description: A geothermal energy company that generates electricity through enhanced geothermal systems (EGS) using horizontal drilling techniques. The company develops and operates next-generation geothermal projects including Project Red and Cape Station.
verified.notes: Source [0] (company website) indicates IPO launch announced May 4, 2026, and registration statement filing announced April 17, 2026, placing company in Pre-IPO stage rather than public. Source [2] (Wikipedia) notes company received $462 million Series E funding in November 2025 led by B Capital. Database entry states 'IPO' but sources show Pre-IPO status as of filing dates in April-May 2026.

changesProposed.name: Nuro

verified.name: Nuro
verified.description: American autonomous vehicle technology company that initially developed custom autonomous delivery vehicles and received the first NHTSA autonomous exemption. Since September 2024, focuses on licensing its proprietary Level 4 driving system, Nuro Driver, to automakers and mobility providers.
verified.notes: Company pivoted from autonomous delivery vehicles to licensing autonomous driving technology in September 2024. Most recent funding: August 2025 Series E closing with $203M at $6B valuation from Uber and Nvidia. Partnership with Uber and Lucid Motors announced July 2025 for 20,000+ robotaxis deployment over six years.

changesProposed.name: Starcloud

current.name: Starcloud
current.founder: Philip Johnston, Ezra Feilden, Adi Oltean

verified.name: Starcloud
verified.description: A United States-based company that designs, builds, and deploys data centers in space using proprietary technology. The company launched its first test satellite, Starcloud-1, equipped with an Nvidia H100 GPU in November 2025.
verified.notes: Company was originally founded as 'Lumen Orbit' in January 2024 in El Segundo, California and rebranded to Starcloud in March 2025 after a legal challenge from Lumen Technologies. Series A announced March 30, 2026, making it the fastest Y Combinator company to reach unicorn status at 17 months post-demo day.

changesProposed.name: Waymo

verified.name: Waymo
verified.description: Autonomous driving technology company operating public commercial robotaxi services in multiple US metropolitan areas. As of March 2026, Waymo operates in 10 US metropolitan areas, provides 500,000 paid rides per week, and has logged 200 million fully autonomous miles.
verified.notes: Wikipedia states Waymo was founded December 13, 2016, tracing origins to Stanford Racing Team (2004) and Google Self-Driving Car Project (January 17, 2009). February 2026 funding round valued company at $126B. Wikipedia indicates 500,000 paid rides per week as of March 2026; database entry references 200K+ paid rides per week which appears outdated. Current stage marked as 'Pre-IPO' as company remains subsidiary of Alphabet with no public IPO announced.
